architecture.summary: TPRU is a recurrent unit that extends standard RNNs (LSTM/GRU) by explicitly incorporating Tensor Product Representations (TPRs) at each time step. At each step, the unit performs structural-role binding and unbinding operations: vector-embedded symbols are bound to vector-embedded structural positions to create distributed representations of symbolic structure. These TPR operations are integrated into the recurrent computation to enable the learning and manipulation of structured representations.

biblio.title: A Simple Recurrent Unit with Reduced Tensor Product Representations

claims.statement: TPRU explicitly executes structural-role binding and unbinding operations to incorporate structural information into learning, compared to LSTMs and GRUs whose ability to learn structured representations is still questionable
Gradient analysis of the proposed TPRU supports the model design choices
Performance on multiple datasets shows the effectiveness of TPRU design choices
Observations on a linguistically grounded study demonstrate the interpretability of TPRU
